page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the average range. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptography and memory operations, with the Passmark CPU encryption test and Passmark memory score both ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain average, as does LLM inference speed for small and medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5ad.12xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with bundled high-speed local NVMe storage, making it a viable alternative to standard memory-heavy instances for workloads that require fast scratch space without massive RAM overhead. The hardware configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, batch processing, software compilation, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe SSDs and strong cryptographic performance.
description: The Amazon Web Services c5ad.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals a tradeoff, with poor single-core performance but average multi-core execution and strong performance in cryptographic and memory operations, ranking in the top 25% for Passmark encryption and memory tests. Lacking a GPU, this instance is optimized for CPU-bound, multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for batch processing, software compilation, database operations, and web serving workloads that require high-speed local scratch storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs, fast DDR4 memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong performance in cryptographic and memory benchmarks
Features a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for multi-threaded batch processing, databases, and software compilation
